findings.claim: Quotation-mark convention drifts from the declared AmE house style.
findings.evidence: generator_meta declares spelling_variety 'AmE' and fictional_source 'AmE house style', yet all dialogue is set in single quotes: 'People have spent a hundred years telling us these coasts are finished,' and 'We can green any dry shore the fog reaches...'. Single quotation marks for primary speech are a British convention; AmE house style uses double quotes.
findings.why: Minor, non-verdict-changing: prose vocabulary and spelling ('liters','fibers') are consistently AmE and the CLEAR verdict on calque/agreement/spelling stands; this is a punctuation/house-style consistency note typically normalized at render, not a language-quality defect.
